explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.findings: Documentation mentions physician-audited datasets and safety checks, including testing and examination. It also references regulatory requirements and ethical considerations.
external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Publish third-party audit results, certifications, or regulatory compliance documentation.

relevant_sources.summary: This blog post, "Introducing Oswell AI Health Agent," provides evidence for external accountability, governance, oversight, privacy, and transparency. It supports external accountability and oversight by mentioning physician-audited datasets and safety checks like testing and examination. The post also touches on governance and oversight through mentions of audits and reviews, and supports privacy by describing data use for personalization. Transparency is evidenced by the mention of conversational AI and OpenAI-powered agents.
relevant_sources.title: Introducing Oswell AI Health Agent

relevant_sources.summary: The "Language Models at Oscar" document provides evidence for **governance**, **transparency**, and **external accountability**. It details an AI governance process for LLMs, addressing accuracy, performance, and regulatory/ethical considerations, which supports governance. The document also mentions accessible tools for testing OpenAI models, indicating operational use and transparency in their capabilities and limitations, and an iterative development approach, further supporting transparency and governance. Finally, the explicit mention of regulatory requirements and ethical considerations points to the document's support for external accountability.
relevant_sources.title: Language Models at Oscar

summary.overall_findings: Oscar Health's public disclosures address governance and oversight, with materials detailing an AI governance process for LLMs and mentioning physician-audited datasets. Overall, 5 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars have documented evidence, including transparency, where disclosures reference conversational AI and OpenAI-powered agents. Further, published materials describe data use for personalization under privacy and reference safety checks, including testing and examination, for external accountability. No qualifying public evidence was found for fairness or explainability, based on a review of 16 publicly available sources.
